Hero Hook: French illustrator in Annecy: bold flat color, minimal figures, childhood & silence—digital for global brands, acrylic for personal canvases.
Active Since: Graphic studio founded 2006 (Lyon, per LM); full-time illustration with Creasenso ~10 years as of Mar 2022; Emile-Cohl (Lyon) arts appliqués
Story (Full Text):
Jérôme Masi is a freelance art director and illustrator based in Annecy, France, represented by Creasenso. In a 2022 LM magazine interview he traced his path from École Emile-Cohl in Lyon into video games, then founding a graphic studio in Lyon in 2006, moving through a collective and motion design before committing full-time to illustration.

Commercial work stays largely digital for clients he names in interview—such as Orange, Tissot, and British Airways—while personal pieces often return to acrylic on canvas in a dedicated studio area, moving between desk and easel.

He describes his own taste as sober and minimal; the cover work “Childhood” embodies silence, interiority, and childhood, and he wants viewers to fill the intentional gaps in the image themselves.

Street Collector fits artists whose graphic language reads clearly at lamp scale: his flat color, strong silhouettes, and restrained palettes translate well to a small, lit surface without losing identity.
Pull Quote: « J'ai envie que chaque personne remplisse à sa façon les vides, les questions laissées sans réponse dans cette image. » — Jérôme Masi, LM magazine, 2022
Exhibitions (Text List):
2022 — Group exhibition, Taiwan (stated March 2022 interview)
2022 — Exhibition beginning April, Hangzhou, China (stated March 2022 interview)
Press (Text + Links):
LM magazine — 2022 — Interview (French) covering Annecy practice, clients, and Taiwan / Hangzhou exhibitions — https://www.lm-magazine.com/blog/2022/03/01/jerome-masi-2/
Creasenso — portfolio listing — https://www.creasenso.com/en/portfolios/image/illustration/jerome-masi
Notes: Instagram post image CDN URLs not auto-filled—add 4 process images only from verified @jerome.masi post permalinks. Internal doc artists.md lists Instagram as jerome.masi; matches user list.

Hero Hook: Cologne illustrator: flat, saturated vector scenes; broke out via “One Icon a Day,” Adobe MAX 2016, and Adobe Stock—bold work built for screens and products.
Active Since: Freelance illustration from 2016 (Adobe blog); prior study Mediendesign Rheinische Fachhochschule Köln
Story (Full Text):
Moritz Adam Schmitt is a Cologne-based illustrator and digital artist. Adobe’s 2017 “New Face” feature documents his self-taught push into illustration via the year-long “One Icon a Day” Instagram project, rapid audience growth, invitation to Adobe MAX 2016, and freelance practice from early 2016.

His interview emphasizes research, reference gathering, iPad roughs in Photoshop Sketch, then vector refinement—rooted in Adobe Creative Cloud workflows.

Influences cited in the same piece include street and pop art sensibilities and peers on Behance and Instagram; commercially he is linked to bold flat color and accessible iconography.

Street Collector: high-contrast flat illustration tends to read instantly on a small lamp panel, aligning with the brand’s need for legible, collectible graphics.
Pull Quote: „Ich lasse mich gerne inspirieren, stöbere auf Behance oder auf den Kanälen anderer Künstler. Die Community ist ein Sammelbecken kreativer Ideen und Impressionen.“ — Moritz Adam Schmitt, Adobe Blog, 2017
Press (Text + Links): Adobe Blog (DE) — 2017 — “Adobe Stock New Face: Illustrator Moritz Adam Schmitt” — https://blog.adobe.com/de/publish/2017/12/07/new-face-illustrator-moritz-adam-schmitt

Hero Hook: Dutch duo painting hyper-detailed (sur)realistic murals; they shoot their own reference so every image is autonomous. Name nods to a toxic-material paint storeroom.
Location: Netherlands (duo: Niels van Swaemen, b. 1981 Eindhoven; Kaspar van Leek, b. 1983 Philipsburg, Sint Maarten)
Active Since: Collaborating since 2007; dedicated to murals full-time from 2012 (studiogiftig.nl/about)
Story (Full Text):
Studio Giftig is the Netherlands-based duo of Niels van Swaemen (born 1981, Eindhoven) and Kaspar van Leek (born 1983, Philipsburg, Sint Maarten). Their official English About page states they met in 2007 when both were already active individually in street art.

They worked from a vacant warehouse that included a storage room for toxic substances and chemicals where they kept paint and spray cans; "Studio Giftig" takes its name from that room because giftig means toxic in Dutch.

At first one member studied at art school while the other worked as a graphic designer, so projects ran evenings and weekends; in 2012 they committed full time to murals and refined a shared, highly detailed (sur)realistic style.

A defining process claim on the same site is that all images are autonomously designed: they photograph their own objects and models to maintain total control of their visual language before painting.

Third-party quotes reproduced on studiogiftig.nl include Amsterdam Street Art and Inside Information praising their mural quality and conceptual motivation; their CV page lists international mural festivals and awards such as Street Art Cities "Best mural worldwide 2022" and Dutch Street Art Awards.
Pull Quote: Studio Giftig creates murals on the highest level with top quality. They speak their own visual language. Expect a well-motivated concept with their distinctive photo realistic style. — Amsterdam Street Art (quoted on studiogiftig.nl/about)
Impact Callout: Street Art Cities Best mural worldwide 2022 and Dutch Street Art Awards recognition appear on the official CV.
Exclusive Callout: Autonomous image pipeline (own photography of models/objects) is the differentiator emphasized on the official About page.
Exhibitions (Text List): Official CV at studiogiftig.nl/cv lists selected murals, festivals, and awards internationally (Europe and beyond).
Press (Text + Links): Amsterdam Street Art and Inside Information quotes are embedded on studiogiftig.nl/about; Amsterdam Street Art also tags coverage of the duo.
Notes: All biographical dates and the "toxic storeroom" naming story are verbatim themes from the official bilingual About page.

Hero Hook: Multidisciplinary designer and artist behind @unapaulogetic_: motion, illustration, typography, and experimental projects like "Capturing Chaos," documented on paulrozenboim.com.

Story (Full Text):
Retail and portfolio pages tie the Instagram handle @unapaulogetic_ to Paul Rozenboim, described as a Colombia-born multidisciplinary visual designer based in Tel Aviv, with work spanning motion design, illustration, typography, and installations.

His site paulrozenboim.com hosts project case studies such as "Capturing Chaos," which explains recording a double pendulum’s motion and translating it into 3D extrusions in Cinema 4D to produce sculptural stills and video art.

Another documented project, "Bring your Imagination to Life," references participation in the LaCulture Typographics Exhibition, signaling a practice that crosses gallery-oriented typography and digital craft.

The Street Lamp lists a collectible edition titled "Hole" attributed to Unapaulogetic, connecting the handle to limited-run art merchandise.

Taken together, sources emphasize generative systems, 3D motion workflows, and typographic exhibition history rather than a single static medium.
Pull Quote: The double pendulum, a simple yet chaotic system, generates unpredictable motion paths that never repeat. — Project description, Paul Rozenboim, "Capturing Chaos" (paulrozenboim.com/capturing-chaos)
Impact Callout: Work linked from a branded collectible storefront and standalone project sites with documented generative/3D process.
Exclusive Callout: Verify any "exclusive" drops against The Street Lamp product copy and the artist’s own site—not third-party aggregators alone.

Notes: Instagram handle confirmed on paulrozenboim.com ("Follow Me @unapaulogetic_"). Biographical "Colombia-born" appears on The Street Lamp; cross-check for future edits.

Story (Full Text):
Hiroyasu Tsuri’s official biography states he was born in Yokohama, is known as TWOONE for large-scale murals, and shows in institutions including the National Gallery of Australia, Straat Museum (Amsterdam), and MUCA (Munich).

It recounts relocating to Melbourne in 2003 after high school, immersing in graffiti/street culture, coining "Raw Mark Making," and completing a Swinburne Diploma of Visual Arts & Multimedia in 2009.

Australia granted permanent residency in 2010 via a Distinguished Talent Visa the same year he joined Backwoods Gallery representation.

From 2014 Berlin served as a European hub for mural and performance collaborations; from 2021 he also bases studio practice in Sant’Elia a Pianisi, Italy.

The on-site CV enumerates solo shows from 2008’s "Thousand Can Show" through 2025 Melbourne and Nagoya projects, plus public murals in Australia, Japan, Italy, Canada, and elsewhere with linked Instagram documentation.
